Q1: Since the benchmark is sourced from WHO articles it will be difficult to expect deployment need to include other document formats, so i think it would be nice if primarily focus  health content.
Q2: Since the benchmark is only sourced from WHO articles it might not contain local specific contents, however most models are expected to preform in general translation tasks like translating dates. For Endemic disease i believe models trained on generic health texts are able to translate the contents to target language like Amharic
Q3: Since health domain is very sensitive domain,  ground truth translation by clinical translators trained by the Ministry of Health should be authoritative but incase if we don't have specific trained by MOH i think we can also consider using translations by Amharic-speaking physicians since they are familiar with the domain and speaks the target language.
Q4: I believe it is important to consider different metrics in addition to  using automatic metrics known in MT evaluation. I would consider results automatic metrics  (MT), faithfulness to source meaning, terminological consistency and compliance with Ministry of Health-approved glossaries for success criteria. There are approved glossaries by Ministry of Health (MOH) of Ethiopia and  Ethiopian Food and Drug Authority (EFDA) in Ethiopia if needed.
